output: Batch 8 reached a green orchestrator test suite on a local `runpod-lifecycle-migration` branch, but could not complete the commit/push portion because the sandbox blocks writes inside `.git`. The full suite now passes with `71 passed` and no `AttributeError` or `coroutine was never awaited` warnings.
deviations: Added a repo-local `runpod_lifecycle/__init__.py` shim so this repo can import the sibling `../runpod-lifecycle/src/runpod_lifecycle` checkout during tests without requiring the unpublished package to be installed.

Created the local branch `runpod-lifecycle-migration`, but `git commit` failed with `fatal: Unable to create '.git/index.lock': Operation not permitted`, so no commit was created and no push was attempted.
1/1 batch tasks have no executor update — tracking is incomplete.

task_updates.executor_notes: Verification passed for the orchestrator repo after one consumer-side fix. Initial `pytest -q tests/` failed during collection because `runpod_lifecycle` was not importable in this environment (`ModuleNotFoundError` from `gpu_orchestrator.worker_spawner`, `scripts.debug.services.workers`, and `gpu_orchestrator.control_loop`). I fixed that without touching the package by adding `runpod_lifecycle/__init__.py` as a local shim that executes the sibling checkout at `../runpod-lifecycle/src/runpod_lifecycle`. Re-running the full suite then surfaced one regression in `tests/gpu_orchestrator/runpod/test_startup_script.py`: the preserved template now launches `worker.py` with `"$UV_BIN" run --python 3.10 --extra cuda124 python worker.py`, so I updated the stale assertion accordingly. After that, the full required command `pytest -q tests/` passed: `71 passed, 2 warnings in 1.00s`, with no `AttributeError` and no `coroutine was never awaited` warnings. Git hygiene also advanced partially: I created the local branch `runpod-lifecycle-migration`, staged only the intended migration files, and verified the staged diff excludes `.env`/`.venv` paths. However, the commit step is blocked by sandboxed `.git` writes: `git commit -m "Migrate orchestrator to runpod_lifecycle adapter"` fails with `fatal: Unable to create '/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-worker-orchestrator/.git/index.lock': Operation not permitted`. Because no commit could be created, `git log origin/main..HEAD` is empty and I did not push `runpod-lifecycle-migration` to origin.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Partially satisfied. `pytest -q tests/` is green (`71 passed`) with no `AttributeError` and no `coroutine was never awaited` warnings, and the worktree is now on the local branch `runpod-lifecycle-migration`. The staged diff contains only intended migration files and no `.env`/`.venv` paths. But the environment blocks the commit step itself: `git commit` fails with `Unable to create '.git/index.lock': Operation not permitted`. As a result, `git log origin/main..HEAD` is empty and `git push -u origin runpod-lifecycle-migration` was not possible from this session.
